summary: Second-round external-review follow-up for CC-0065 HTTPRoute sub-reconciler. Addresses two sourcery-ai findings: (1) removed silent hostname fallback in keystoneStatusEndpoint so misconfiguration surfaces as https:///v3 rather than being masked by the cluster-local URL; (2) added leading-slash normalization for spec.gateway.path with a dedicated unit test; (3) converted the REQ-008 NetworkPolicy gateway-peer block into an explicit REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT explaining why the auto-appended peer is retained. Build, go vet, golangci-lint, and the full controller test suite all pass. However the refactored networkpolicy comment reintroduces the exact anti-pattern the external reviewer asked to remove (a trailing 'Reviewer: please verify' style note), and it is written in German — violating the blocking English-only language rule.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (go test ./internal/controller/... -count=1 = ok)
Edge cases are covered (empty path, leading-slash-missing path, custom path, gateway-nil endpoint, gateway-set endpoint, cross-namespace gateway peer, default-namespace gateway peer)
New behavior (path normalization) has a dedicated test TestBuildKeystoneHTTPRoute_PathWithoutLeadingSlash_Normalized

Endpoint-fallback removal has no explicit negative test (e.g. gateway set with empty hostname produces https:///v3) — acceptable because webhook+CRD MinLength=1 guarantees non-empty, and adding a test would lock in unreachable state

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (reconcileNetworkPolicy / reconcileHPA sub-reconciler contract, apiequality spec comparison, commonLabels helper)
Comments explain WHY not WHAT (good: hostname fallback rationale at reconcile_httproute.go:45-52; good: path normalization rationale at reconcile_httproute.go:147-150)
No dead code or commented-out alternatives

security_checklist.item: All user inputs validated (hostname MinLength=1, parentRef.name MinLength=1, webhook REQ-007 defense-in-depth)
No path traversal (spec.gateway.path is used only as an HTTPRoute PathPrefix match value, not a filesystem path)
REQ-008 auto-appended Gateway-namespace ingress peer widens the surface of spec.networkPolicy by a full namespace — documented tradeoff under REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT, intentional per REQ-008

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible (path normalization is 3 lines; hostname fallback removal is 1 line)

REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT block appropriately documents a deliberate deviation from external reviewer advice — consistent with the 'resolve-ambiguous-commented-out-alternatives-before-merge' pattern when a decision is made explicit

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No speculative generality introduced (normalization is the minimum needed to reject Gateway-controller-invalid paths)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Errors detected early — hostname fallback removal causes status.endpoint to emit visibly broken https:///v3 on post-admission misconfiguration instead of silently returning a cluster-local URL
Path normalization prevents Gateway controllers from silently rejecting the HTTPRoute

defensive_checklist.item: All external inputs validated (hostname, parentRef.name by webhook+CRD)
Defense-in-depth preserved (empty hostname now produces observable broken URL rather than silent fallback)

issues_found.description: Non-English comment introduced. Line 129 contains 'Reviewer: bitte prüfen.' ('bitte prüfen' is German for 'please verify'). This violates critical-rules rule #1 LANGUAGE ('All output in English (code, comments, commits, docs)') whose violation is explicitly 'Non-English output rejected'. The rule is blocking-priority.
issues_found.fix: Delete the trailing 'Reviewer: bitte prüfen.' sentence. The preceding sentence 'What we did instead: kept the behavior and documented the tradeoff.' already closes the block cleanly.
issues_found.evidence:
// out of scope for this review round. — What we did instead: kept the
	// behavior and documented the tradeoff. Reviewer: bitte prüfen.

issues_found.description: The whole point of the second external-review round on CC-0065 was to eliminate the leftover 'Reviewer: please verify.' note from the CC-0065 NetworkPolicy block (documented in .planwerk/review_patterns/resolve-ambiguous-commented-out-alternatives-before-merge.md, occurrence #2). The new comment at line 129 literally reinstates the same anti-pattern, just translated to German ('Reviewer: bitte prüfen.'). Silently reintroducing the pattern defeats the external-review fix and will recur next review round.
issues_found.fix: Remove 'Reviewer: bitte prüfen.' The REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT block already states the decision has been made and documented; any further verification request belongs in PR review comments, not in code.
issues_found.evidence: // behavior and documented the tradeoff. Reviewer: bitte prüfen.

suggested_improvements: Consider shortening the REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT block (reconcile_networkpolicy.go:115-129). At ~15 lines it mixes rationale, counter-argument, and meta ('out of scope for this review round') — once 'Reviewer: bitte prüfen.' is removed, the remaining content would still be clearer if tightened to (a) what the external reviewer suggested, (b) why REQ-008 makes that suggestion infeasible, (c) the tradeoff. Three sentences instead of twelve.
next_steps: Remove the 'Reviewer: bitte prüfen.' sentence from operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_networkpolicy.go:129 — single-line deletion, fully AUTO_FIX.
After the deletion, re-run `go test ./internal/controller/... -count=1` and `golangci-lint run ./internal/controller/...` (both are currently green) and re-request review.
